name: Ix
description: CLI tool that builds a persistent, queryable knowledge graph from codebase analysis. Maps architecture, traces flows, and analyzes change impact — providing directed context to AI tools instead of raw code exploration.
approachDetail: Analyzes code to build a versioned knowledge graph of symbols, relationships, and system boundaries. Four-phase pipeline: Map → Structure → Remember → Understand.
searchDetail: ~14 structured query commands (explain, trace, impact, smell, inventory, rank, etc.) for navigating the knowledge graph
storageDetail: Persistent local knowledge graph storing symbols, relationships, and system boundaries. Versioned and updated incrementally.
mcpDetail: Uses its own plugin system for AI tool integration
toolSupportDetail: Plugin-based integration; Claude plugin available via marketplace
retentionDetail: Knowledge graph persists indefinitely; updated by re-running map
crossMachineDetail: Local-only; graph is stored on the machine where ix runs
tokenBudgetingDetail: Claims 30-99.7% token reduction by providing targeted graph context instead of raw code exploration
contextInjectionDetail: AI tool plugins inject relevant graph context automatically based on the query
networkDetail: Docker required for full analysis functionality; queries against existing graph are local
pros: Significant token reduction via directed context
Structured query commands for architecture exploration
cons: Alpha — APIs and behavior may change
